code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test09-DEC-2016Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals protein and glucose, with cloudy, brown urine and low specific gravity, but no evidence of infection (negative nitrite/leukocytes). Recommend further evaluation of renal function and possible underlying causes (e.g., diabetic nephropathy, tubular dysfunction). Clinical correlation is advised.

coding.display: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
